name: El Hueco de San Blas y La Najarra — circular desde Miraflores de la Sierra
region_intro: Jogging around Miraflores De La Sierra offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains. The region features varied terrain, including shady pine forests, oak forests, and mountain trails. Situated on the southern slopes of Pico de la Pala and near the Najarra mountain slopes, it provides numerous routes through the Guadarrama mountains. The natural environment is part of protected areas, including the National Park of the Sierra del Guadarrama and a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Best jogging routes around Miraflores De La Sierra The most popular jogging route is Cerro de San Pedro from Alto del Mojón, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 47 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain for its length. Another top favourite among local runners is El Hueco de San Blas y La Najarra — circular desde Miraflores de la Sierra, a difficult 13.4 miles (21.6 km) path. This challenging circular route features substantial elevation gain through mountain terrain. Local runners also love the San Blas Hollow Loop, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, often completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes. Jogging around Miraflores De La Sierra is defined by mountain trails, pine and oak forests, and riverside paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to difficult mountain expeditions. The routes in Miraflores De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore Miraflores De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: Miraflores De La Sierra offers a superb variety of landscapes for running. You'll find shady &lt;strong&gt;Monte Aguirre pine forests&lt;/strong&gt;, especially pleasant in summer, and diverse &lt;strong&gt;oak forests&lt;/strong&gt; like the Muñequilla. The region is also characterized by numerous &lt;strong&gt;mountain trails&lt;/strong&gt;, ranging from gentle paths to demanding climbs, and picturesque &lt;strong&gt;riverside paths&lt;/strong&gt; leading to the Miraflores de la Sierra reservoir.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: While many routes in Miraflores De La Sierra are rated as difficult due to significant elevation gain, there are options for those seeking a moderate challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1172441123/cerro-de-san-pedro-from-alto-del-mojon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de San Pedro from Alto del Mojón&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter route at 4.4 km (2.7 miles) that can be a good jog, though it still includes some elevation. The area generally caters to a range of fitness levels, so you can find trails that suit your experience.

Q: How many running routes are available in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: There are over 30 running routes available around Miraflores De La Sierra, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulties. These include 7 moderate routes and 31 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every runner.

Q: What makes the running trails in Miraflores De La Sierra unique?
A: The running trails in Miraflores De La Sierra are unique due to their location within protected natural areas, including the &lt;strong&gt;National Park of the Sierra del Guadarrama&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Regional Park of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares&lt;/strong&gt;, which is also a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. This means you'll be running amidst rich biodiversity, varied landscapes, and even historical elements like old sheepfolds and charcoal kilns, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: Can I find routes with waterfalls or other natural landmarks in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: Yes, several running routes in the area pass by beautiful natural landmarks. You can explore trails that lead to stunning waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/851377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purgatory Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6317937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Coso Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, some routes offer views of reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/484448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the running trails?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the natural beauty, many trails in Miraflores De La Sierra pass by vestiges of past human activity. You might encounter old &lt;strong&gt;sheepfolds&lt;/strong&gt;, &lt;strong&gt;shepherd huts&lt;/strong&gt;, ancient &lt;strong&gt;charcoal kilns&lt;/strong&gt;, and dry stone walls. The &lt;strong&gt;Fuente del Cura Recreation Area&lt;/strong&gt;, surrounded by oak trees, is also a notable spot and a starting point for some routes, including the one to San Blas Shrine.

Q: Are there options for circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, Miraflores De La Sierra offers several excellent circular running routes. A popular choice is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233965571/el-hueco-de-san-blas-y-la-najarra-circular-desde-miraflores-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hueco de San Blas y La Najarra — circular desde Miraflores de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 21.6 km (13.4 miles) with significant elevation. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232818468/san-blas-hollow-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Blas Hollow Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.1 km (7.5 miles) trail through the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: The best time for running in Miraflores De La Sierra depends on your preference. The varied terrain changes beautifully with each season. &lt;strong&gt;Summer&lt;/strong&gt; offers pleasant runs through shady pine forests like Monte Aguirre. In &lt;strong&gt;winter&lt;/strong&gt;, you can experience the striking, snow-covered peaks of the Cuerda Larga, though some routes may require more specialized gear and caution. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and vibrant scenery.

Q: Are the running trails in Miraflores De La Sierra suitable for families?
A: While the guide focuses on jogging routes, the region offers a mix of trails. Some of the shorter, less strenuous paths, particularly those through oak forests or by the river, could be suitable for families looking for a gentle run or walk. However, many of the listed jogging routes involve significant elevation and distance, so it's best to check individual route details for family suitability.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: The running routes in Miraflores De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 8 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, the challenge of the mountain trails, and the opportunity to explore protected natural areas. The variety of terrain, from shady forests to open mountain paths, is frequently highlighted as a major draw.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Miraflores De La Sierra is an excellent destination for experienced runners seeking a challenge. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233965571/el-hueco-de-san-blas-y-la-najarra-circular-desde-miraflores-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hueco de San Blas y La Najarra — circular desde Miraflores de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; (21.6 km / 13.4 miles with significant elevation) and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14884351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Morcuera loop from Miraflores de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; (18.6 km / 11.6 miles) offer strenuous workouts through mountain terrain, perfect for those looking for a demanding expedition.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: Miraflores De La Sierra is part of the National Park of the Sierra del Guadarrama and the Regional Park of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares. While many trails in natural parks are dog-friendly, specific regulations regarding leashes or restricted areas may apply to protect wildlife. It's always advisable to check the current rules for the specific section of the park you plan to visit to ensure a pleasant and compliant run with your dog.
tips: This natural enclave is located in the municipalities of Manzanares el Real and Soto del Real. Beautiful views.

La Najarra (2122 m) is a mountain located in the Sierra de Guadarrama, a mountain range in the Community of Madrid.
You can access La Najarra from several points, depending on the route you choose. Some popular routes start from nearby towns such as Miraflores de la Sierra or Soto del Real. Some are more demanding than others, so choose the one that suits your skill level.
Enjoy the beautiful panoramic views from the summit of La Najarra.

It is located in the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range, between the municipalities of Miraflores de la Sierra and Rascafría, in the Community of Madrid.

From Miraflores de la Sierra (1200m), the route ascends approximately 600m over 10km (average gradient 4–6%), and after reaching the summit, it descends 13km to Rascafría (1150m) with a gradient of 4.5–7%.

The path goes through a beautiful grove of large oak trees, passes the Umbría de la Raya hut and climbs up to the pastures of Parada del Rey. On the left-hand side, on the Peña de la Iruela hill, a group of huddled and solitary Pyrenean oaks stands out. The most unique aspect is the huge branches that scatter and dance in all directions. La Parada del Rey is a traditional resting place for the herds that roamed the mountains, a place rich in pastures to feed before facing the hard journey of the Altos de la Morcuera. The cattle trails have also been used as communication routes, and numerous current highways have even been built on them. The Cordel del Puerto de la Morcuera is on the old Camino Viejo from Madrid to El Paular, also called Camino Real de Rascafría, and the carriages and cavalry used the meadows of the Parada del Rey to rest on the journey through the mountains. On one occasion, Isabel del Borbón, wife of Felipe IV, was traveling from Madrid to the El Paular charterhouse and the royal entourage stopped at the resting place at Peña de la Iruela. At that time the name of the municipality was Porquerizas de la Sierra, since the 13th century, when it was repopulated by Segovian farmers who were dedicated to raising pigs. The queen was contemplating the landscape and was impressed by the amount of flowers that grew in those meadows, immediately ordering the name of the town to be changed to Miraflores de la Sierra. At that time, the mountain village exalted the local place name and the area of the pastoral glen is called Parada del Rey.

waypoints.name: Oaks of the Parada del Rey
